Off Calendar Jul-16-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Theodore Broomfield, 415.500.2029 for Plaintiffs Alsonso Huerta-Oliver et al. (Zoom)
Steve Abern, 510.763.2324 for Defendants Viking Services Corporation et al. (Zoom)
Rena Sera, 415.398.0200 for Defendants 320 Alemnay LLC et al.

Argued by Plantiff. Plantiff's request for a continuance is denied. The Court states that the Plaintiff does not meet the burden per CCP 437c(h) to show that facts could be presented to oppose the motion.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT VIKING SERVICES CORPORATION, QUANG SAU MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is GRANTED. Plaintiffs do not dispute that the moving defendants are property managers and agents of the owner/landlord. By its express terms, Civil Code 1942.4 imposes duties on and provides for a claim against a landlord. Plaintiff fails to cite, nor is the court aware of, any authority that a property manager may be held liable under section. 1942.4. Stoiber v. Honeychuck (1980) 101 Cal. App. 3d 903, 930 is inapposite because that decision does not discuss section 1942.4. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)
